resources.string: A serious privacy messenger with real end-to-end encryption by default and built-in censorship bypass. No phone, no email, no ads.
Your identity is a numeric UIN and nothing else. The account isn\'t tied to you. Burn it and start over whenever you want.
Radio Chat finds people near you over Bluetooth and Wi-Fi Direct. Messages hop device to device, with no servers and no cell signal needed.
End-to-end encrypted on every message. The server doesn\'t see who\'s writing to whom: the sender\'s identity is sealed inside the envelope, not in the header.
A PIN locks the app and encrypts your message history on-device, with optional fingerprint, plus decoy and wipe PINs for when you\'re forced to unlock.
Online, Away, DND, Invisible, Offline. The classic five, and they actually mean something. Tap your status icon to switch.
RCQ isn\'t one company\'s server. Every server is an independent island, and anyone can run one. Your account lives on the island you pick, and you keep your number if you move.

When the network blocks RCQ, the app routes itself through a relay, disguised as ordinary traffic. The relay network is community-run, and onion routing chains it so no single relay sees both you and your island.
Creates a new anonymous identity alongside your current one. Leave the server blank to use the public server.
Accounts on this device. Switch between them from the account button on the home screen. Deleting an account here removes it from this device only — its identity stays on the server.
Removes this account and its messages from this device. The account stays on the server and can be added again later.
Add a friend by their UIN, or share your QR code to get started.
%1$s is coming to the Android app soon.
RCQ closed unexpectedly last time. Send a technical report (the error and your device model only, no message content) so we can fix it?
You can close this, the download keeps going. The installer opens when it is ready.
This will log the website in as your account so you can chat from a browser. Only continue if you opened this from the QR code on chat.rcq.app yourself.
Web connected. Your browser should log in now.
Couldn\'t connect to web. The code may have expired — try again.
This link was already used. Refresh the QR on the website and scan the new one.
Couldn\'t add the contact. Check the connection and try again.

Automatic backup, not a second login — you never switch or sign in there. While your main island works, a copy of each incoming message is also dropped on the backup. If your main island is blocked or goes down, the app keeps receiving from the backup on its own. Your number on a backup island may be a different one (islands number independently); your main number never changes.
Keep a backup on another island
The island is picked automatically from the public catalogue
No reachable backup island right now. Try again later, or add one manually.
The island list is signed with your key and published, so contacts on the latest app versions deliver to every island automatically. Only this device polls the backup mailbox.
That does not look like an island host.
That is already your primary island.
Could not connect to the island.

The app will switch to %1$s: your number and address become the ones from that island, and the current primary island becomes a backup. Chat history stays on this device. Contacts will see a routine security-code change.
Browsers connected to your account. To connect one, open chat.rcq.app, choose Connect phone, and scan the code with your camera.

1. On your computer, open chat.rcq.app
2. Choose "Connect phone"
3. Point your phone's camera at the QR code shown there

Your phone will ask you to confirm.
Write these words down and keep them somewhere safe and offline. Anyone with this phrase can take over your account. We can\'t recover it for you.
To restore later: reinstall RCQ, tap \"Restore from phrase\" and enter these words.
This account was created before recovery phrases existed, so there is no phrase for it. New accounts get one automatically.
Enter your PIN to view the recovery phrase.
Generate a brand-new identity and recovery phrase while keeping the same UIN. Use this only if you think your keys may have been compromised.

Your old recovery phrase stops working and is replaced by a new one, so write the new one down. Your contacts will see a \"security code changed\" warning, and messages sent to you during the switch may not arrive. Your UIN stays the same.
Keys re-issued. Write down your new recovery phrase.
Couldn\'t re-issue keys. Check your connection and try again.
Enter your recovery phrase (24 words, or 48 for an older account) to restore your account on this device.
That doesn\'t look like a valid 24-word recovery phrase.
No account on this server matches that phrase.
Couldn\'t restore. Check your connection and the phrase.
A PIN encrypts your message history on this device and is required every time you open the app. Keep it safe: there is no recovery if you forget it.
How long the app can stay in the background before it asks for the PIN again. Longer is more convenient but less private on a shared device.
PIN must be at least 4 digits
Entering this PIN instead of your real one erases all RCQ data on this device and resets the app. There is no confirmation and no undo. Use it if you are forced to unlock.
Pick a different PIN from your real one.
Entering this PIN instead of your real one opens the app showing only the account you pick below. Your other accounts stay hidden until you enter your real PIN.
Your messages are locked on this device.
Too many attempts. Try again in %1$d s.
Off while a duress PIN is set
Off while biometric unlock is on
Confirm it\'s you to open your messages.
Confirm your fingerprint or face to turn it on.
Microphone access is needed for calls (and camera for video).
Finds people nearby over Bluetooth and connects directly over Wi-Fi. No internet, no servers. Works between Android devices only.
Bluetooth and Wi-Fi permissions are needed for Radio
Microphone permission is needed to talk
Turn on Bluetooth to use Radio
This device doesn\'t support Wi-Fi Direct
No rooms yet. Create one or join with a key.
You\'re not a member of this room.
Location permission is needed to discover people nearby.
Couldn\'t get your location. Try again.
Only a coarse ~1km area is shared, never your exact location. You appear only while sharing.
Payment is mocked in this build.
Describe what went wrong. Your platform and app version are attached automatically.
Pick any free 3-9 digit UIN. Shorter UINs are rarer and pricier.
Your UIN is your handle in RCQ, a number tied to no phone or email. Other users find you by it; shorter UINs are scarcer, so they cost more.

Contacts, groups, profile and audio rooms move with you onto the new UIN. The old UIN returns to the shared pool and may be claimed by someone else later. Your local chat history stays on this device.
We\'ll migrate your account onto this UIN. Contacts, groups and your chat history move with you; the old UIN frees up.
Someone grabbed it first. Try a different UIN.
Choose light, dark, or follow the system theme.
Pick a different launcher icon. The home screen may flicker for a moment while it updates.
Control who can see you, who can reach you, and which server you\'re on.
Removes messages from this device only. Your account and contacts stay.
Get a fresh UIN. Your contacts, groups, keys and chat history are all kept.
Removes all messages from this device. Your account and contacts stay.
You get a freshly-allocated UIN. Your contacts, groups, keys and chat history on this device are all kept.
This deletes your account and all local data. It cannot be undone.
End-to-end encrypted. Sealed sender. Open source.
People who opened your profile in the last 7 days.
Comma-separated, e.g. music, hiking, retro tech.
Who can see when you were last online.
Keep showing as recently online for a while after the app closes.
You keep showing as online for this long after you close the app. Change it in Settings, Privacy & Network.
Your network looked blocked, so RCQ is connecting through its built-in encrypted bypass. No setup needed, this turns on automatically when the direct connection fails.
Who can open your full profile (city, age, about).
Who can see your gender on your profile.
Who can add me to groups
Who is allowed to add you to a group.
Who sees your blue ticks. Turning this off hides theirs too.
Blocks screenshots and screen recording, and hides the app in the recent-apps switcher. A determined person can still photograph the screen with another device.
Allow being considered for the public Hall of Fame at rcq.app. Only your nickname and UIN, and only if approved. You can turn this off any time.
Image is too large. Pick a smaller one (max ~256 KB).
Could not upload the image. Try again.
Routes the connection through a relay when the network blocks RCQ directly. Improves reliability on restrictive networks. Takes effect on next launch.
Routes through two relays so no single relay sees both you and the server. Turns on the protected connection automatically. A little slower. Takes effect on next launch.
If the server is blocked directly but reachable on the current route, the relay is doing its job. If both fail, your network is blocking everything we can reach.
Master switch for all app tones. Incoming calls still ring.
Play a tone when a new message arrives in a chat you don\'t have open.
Play a tone when one of your contacts comes online or goes offline.

Tone volume follows the media volume (the volume buttons). Tones stay quiet in silent, vibrate and Do Not Disturb modes. Per-chat mute (long-press a chat on the home screen) silences message sounds for that chat.
Translations roll out gradually. Untranslated text falls back to English.
Per-chat mute is available from a long-press on the home screen. System push delivery is configured in Android Settings.
Couldn\'t reach that server. Nothing changed.
Add an account on an organisation\'s island or your own self-hosted RCQ server. Your current account stays on this device.
Only needed for closed servers that require an invite. Leave blank for open ones.
This adds a new account on that server. Your current account stays on this device with all its chats and contacts; switch between them from the account menu.
Adds a new account on %1$s. Your current account on %2$s stays on this device, and you can switch back anytime.
Adds a new account on %1$s. Your current account on %2$s stays on this device.
New members can\'t self-join via search
Only you see the full roster
This deletes the group for everyone.
You\'ll stop receiving messages from this group.
All your contacts are already in this group.
You\'ll both be removed from each other\'s contacts and future messages from this UIN are dropped.
This contact hasn\'t published forward-secrecy keys yet, so there\'s nothing to verify.
Compare this number with your contact over a channel you trust, in person or on a call. If it matches on both phones, no one is intercepting your messages.
This contact\'s safety number changed. It could be a new device, or someone intercepting. Open it to verify again.
You\'ll be paired with a random person for an anonymous 5-minute chat. Be kind. 18+ only.
Set your age in your profile first (18+ only).
Random chat is for users 18 and older.
Only the owner can post in this group.
You shared a way to connect
Hand this contact a relay so they can get through when their own connections are blocked. They tap Add to use it.
No relays available to share yet.
Paste an rcq-relay:// link you received out of band.
That isn\'t a valid relay link.
No shared relays. A contact can send you one in chat, or paste a link above.
Joining registers your key on %1$s so the group can deliver to you.
You have no groups to share.
Invite-only group. Ask a member to add you.
Couldn\'t join. The island may be unreachable or the group is invite-only.
Tap the preview to hide it as a spoiler.
Hidden. The recipient taps to reveal it.
Have a friend scan this to add you.
Add me on RCQ. My UIN: %1$s\n%2$s
Some members don\'t accept group invites from you.
Couldn\'t create the group. Try again.
Search for people and groups to add. A bare number is on your island; for another island type number@island.
This is you — your account on this island
The group owner has blocked this user.
This user only accepts group invites from their contacts.
This user doesn\'t accept group invites.
Couldn\'t add this member. Try again.
